summary: Tarsi's conjecture that the union of a forest (1-degenerate graph) and a 2-degenerate graph is 5-colorable appears to remain open. No post-2013 paper resolving or substantially advancing the conjecture was found across six targeted searches covering arXiv, web-wide queries, and author-specific terms. The best-known upper bound remains 6, obtained by taking the product of the individual optimal colorings (2-coloring of the forest and 3-coloring of the 2-degenerate graph), and the lower bound $K_5$ shows 5 would be tight.
search_queries: coloring union degenerate graphs conjecture graph theory arXiv
chromatic number union forest 2-degenerate 5-coloring graph theory 2015 2020
arXiv "union of a forest" "2-degenerate" chromatic coloring conjecture
Schönheim Klein "m-composed" graphs coloring conjecture proof 2014 2015 2016 2017 2018
"m-degenerate" OR "k-degenerate" graph union coloring "5-coloring" OR "5-colorable" conjecture Tarsi 2014 2015 2016 2017 2018 2019 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024 2025
notes: The OPG problem page (http://www.openproblemgarden.org/op/coloring_the_union_of_degenerate_graphs) returned ECONNREFUSED and could not be fetched directly, so any post-2013 discussion or bibliography updates there were not visible. The Wesleyan thesis PDF (2024) retrieved from Wesleyan's digital collections was unreadable binary data; it might discuss the conjecture but could not be verified. The arXiv paper 2601.15245 ('Coloring small locally sparse degenerate graphs and related problems', January 2026) deals with related degeneracy-coloring questions but does not address this specific conjecture. No other post-2013 candidate paper was found, consistent with the conjecture being an unresolved open problem in the literature.
